title: Schoenoplectus lineolatus
taxonomy: Schoenoplectus lineolatus is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family consists of over 5,000 species of aquatic and terrestrial plants. Schoenoplectus lineolatus itself is further categorized within the genus Schoenoplectus, which is a group of tussock-forming sedges. The genus Schoenoplectus is part of the tribe Schoeneae, a large group of sedges that are primarily found in wetland environments. Schoenoplectus lineolatus is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world.
uses: Schoenoplectus lineolatus, also known as beaked club-rush, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. intro: Schoenoplectus lineolatus, also known as the lined bulrush, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Schoenoplectus lineolatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and salves for topical application.
